violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC is scheduled for class and test. PIC to reinstate FMC once food safety certificate is obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety license is expired. PIC to provide proof of enrollment to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025
-Handwashing sinks are lacking proper notification signage. -Chicken was observed thawing in handsink. PIC informed to cease practice. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of the slicer were observed with an accumulation of food debris and apparent rodent droppings. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Establishment must clean and sanitize prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Beans were observed in deep cambro containers. Foods were placed in a shallow manner during time of inspection. Proper Cooling methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

-The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live cockroaches observed during time of inspection, cockroach excrement observed on non-food contact surfaces and live cockroaches and cockroach excrement observed in food. -The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings on food contact surfaces during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Slicers and knifes were observed in the basement. Establishment is not approved for food preparation in the basement due to improper facilities in area. PIC informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black residue. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025
The floors in the prep area were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2026
